name: 6-Hydroxyindole
description: This ingredient is used in permanent dyes as a hair dye. This ingredient has a strong sensitizing ability.In 2006, the SCCS concluded on this ingredient by stating: "The SCCP is of the opinion that the use of 6-hydroxyindole itself as an oxidation dye for hair maximum concentration of 0.5% in the finished cosmetic product (after mixing with hydrogen peroxide) does not present any risk to consumer health, apart from its sensitization potential. Genotoxicity / mutagenicity studies in finished hair dye formulations shall be undertaken in accordance with the relevant advice of the SCCNFP and in accordance with its guidance notes. "

name: Alcohol
description: Alcohol, also called ethanol, is a colorless liquid with a strong odor. It is used in cosmetics for its antimicrobial properties with a broad spectrum (antifungal, antiviral and antibacterial). It is also used in many other cases as a preservative, solvent or to reduce perspiration in deodorants.Although it is not regulated in cosmetics, many cases of possible irritation are nonetheless reported with alcohol (skin, mucous membrane, lung, eyes ...).

name: Alcohol Denat.
description: Denatured alcohol is an alcohol rendered unfit for consumption in order to avoid excise duties (indirect tax levied for example on alcohol, tobacco, or petroleum products) so that it can not be used to create "drinkable alcohols".In cosmetics, it provides a feeling of freshness (aftershave, deodorants or perfumes). It is also used in organic products with essential oils to avoid the use of a preservative and has interesting properties (astringent, solvent ...).Since 1 August 2017, Europe has imposed a very precise denaturing procedure:+ 3 liters of IPA (Isopropyl alcohol / CAS No. 67-63-0)+ 3 liters MEK (methyl ethyl ketone (butanone) / CAS no 78-93-3)+ 1 g of Denatium benzoate (Denatonium benzoate / CAS No. 3734-33-6) must be used per hectolitre of ethanol (pure alcohol).Denat alcohol. is not recommended for very sensitive skin like baby's skin. It can be irritating and tend to dry out the skin.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Allantoin
description: Allantoin comes in the form of a white crystalline powder. It is a nitrogenous compound that is found both in the plant environment (Comfrey root for example) or animal (in the urine of calves). It can also be obtained synthetically from uric acid. In cosmetics, allantoin is used for its astringent, anti-irritant, anti-inflammatory, healing and moisturizing properties. It is also used in the treatment of dentin hypersensitivity. Allantoin is not authorized in organic in France.

name: Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice
description: Aloe Vera gel is contained in the leaves of the plant of the same name. To extract it, simply make a cut at the end and press on it. This gel must be stabilized fairly quickly since in contact with oxygen it degrades rapidly. Aloe Vera Gel has a PH close to that of the skin (4,5), it is an excellent moisturizer, it promotes its elasticity and prevents skin aging. For the scalp, it would help collagen production and delay alopecia (hair loss). Several studies have also shown its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial properties, promoting healing, the treatment of canker sores, gingivitis or oral lichen planus.

name: Alumina
description: Alumina, also known as aluminum oxide, is a naturally occurring mineral found in bauxite. It is a material that has been used for over 40 years for the development of prostheses (including those of the hip). It is also used in dental implants. It is used in whitening type toothpastes as an abrasive agent and thus promotes the removal of dental plaque by friction (with the toothbrush). It is also used for its absorbing properties and its waterproof nature. It also functions as an anti-caking and absorbing agent. It is found in many make-up products such as blush, powder foundation, lipstick and facial cleanser. It is authorized in organic.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Aluminum Chlorohydrate
description: Discovered in 1947, aluminum chlorohydrate has made the antiperspirants previously based on aluminum chloride, less irritating. Nevertheless, this aluminum salt is suspected to be an endocrine disruptor.For the time being, no study proves any relationship between breast cancer in women and aluminum salt. However, it is known that this salt crosses the barriers of the skin, and then end up in different organs: for example in the brain and in particular the hippocampus. It is forbidden in organic.In 2011, the Afssaps (now ANSM) proposed a restriction of the aluminum concentration to 2% in antiperspirants or deodorants. This recommendation has never been followed by Europe.In addition Afssaps recommended not to use antiperspirants containing aluminum on damaged skin, freshly shaved or shaved for example.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Aluminum Starch Octenylsuccinate
description: This aluminium salt is produced by reacting octenylsuccinic anhydride with starch. It is used in cosmetics as an anti-caking agent, a viscosity enhancer (flow resistance) and an absorbent. It is found in products such as lotions and make-up powders. For example, it can prevent foundation from settling on the skin or reduce the oily shine associated with certain lotions. In dry shampoos, it supports the action of a starch, usually rice starch.Although aluminium is considered to be neurotoxic, no study has demonstrated the toxicity of this salt in cosmetics. Note that it is not used in antiperspirants.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Amodimethicone
description: Amodimethicone is a silicone derivative of dimethicone, used mainly in shampoos and hair care for its electrostatic properties. It makes the hair soft, easy to comb and shiny. The advantage of this silicone compared to dimethicone is that, although it is difficult to remove with a shampoo, it prevents other polymers of the same type can "re-deposit" on itself, so the decriated accumulation with dimethicone, which makes hair heavy, no longer exists here.Just like dimethicone, the molecule does not pose any particular problem on human health (directly in any case). However, it is not very biodegradable and its impact on the planet both from the point of view of its manufacture and its rejection in the wild is not negligible.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ammonium Hydroxide

description: Water is the most used ingredient in cosmetics: 83% of products contain it (percentage established from 50,000 products from the Inci Beauty App). Since water is of mineral origin, it can never be certified organic. name: Bee Venom
description: Bee venom, also known as apitoxin, is a bitter, colorless liquid extracted from bees. It is used in cosmetics as an astringent due to the presence of an enzyme called hyaluronidase, and as a skin protection agent due to its anti-inflammatory properties.Bee venom has been used for medicinal purposes since antiquity. It is said to have numerous virtues, including being effective against diseases such as multiple sclerosis and cancer. However, several studies have shown that there is no evidence to support the efficacy of the venom against multiple sclerosis, and no study to date has demonstrated any effect in the prevention or treatment of cancer.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Animal.

name: Behentrimonium Chloride
description: Behentrimonium Chloride is a quaternary ammonium mainly used in cosmetics as an anti-static agent or hair conditioner. For this reason, it is mainly used in hair care products. It is banned in organic farming.In 2010, in an opinion report, the Scientific Committee on Consumer Safety (SCCS) concluded that ‘In addition to the fact that formulations of quaternary ammonium derivatives have an irritant potential, particularly when combinations of the compounds concerned are used, the SCCS indicates that the use of CETRIMONIUM CHLORIDE, STEARTRIMONIUM CHLORIDE and BEHENTRIMONIUM CHLORIDE do not pose a risk to consumer health’ when used within the regulated concentration limits (see below).
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Behentrimonium Methosulfate

name: Benzaldehyde
description: This ingredient is a synthetic aromatic compound with a bitter almond scent that is used in perfumes and scented cosmetic products. It is one of the allergens regulated in Europe.
note: Low penalty in all categories.

name: Benzalkonium Bromide
description: Benzalkonium Bromide — косметический ингредиент. Происхождение: Synthetic.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Behenyl Alcohol
description: Behenyl alcohol or Docosanol is a fatty alcohol. It is used in cosmetics as an emulsifier to keep oily parts well mixed with other liquids. It is also used as a thickener, to increase the foaming capacity of a product or to improve the stability of a foam. It makes the skin soft and its touch remains non-greasy after application. It is authorized in organic.In drugs, docosanol is used as an antiviral against herpes.The CIR (Cosmetic Ingredient Review) in an annual report published in 2008, concluded the safety of fatty alcohols.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Bentonite
description: Bentonite is a clay that usually comes from volcanic ash. It mainly consists of silica and aluminum as well as other minerals, including sodium, magnesium, calcium, iron oxide and potassium. Bentonite clay is used in cosmetics as a texturizer, filler, thickener and pigment binder.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Benzoic Acid
description: Benzoic acid is used as a preservative in cosmetics. It is present naturally in the benzoin gum extracted from "Styrax" type trees, but is "unfortunately" often made in the laboratory from other chemicals. In food, it is used under the name E210. Benzoic acid is allowed in organic. It can irritate the eyes, skin, lungs and digestive tract, but generally does not pose a problem in regulatory concentrations.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Benzophenone-4
description: Benzophenone-4 (Sulisobenzone) is a chemical UV filter that protects the skin from UVB and UVA rays. It is suspected of polluting water and endangering aquatic life.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Benzophenone-5
description: Benzophenone-5 (Sulisobenzone sodium) is a chemical UV filter that protects the skin against UVB and UVA radiation. It is suspected of polluting water and endangering aquatic fauna. It is the sodium salt of Benzophenone-4. Because of the way it is produced, it is associated with an orange flower on our application.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Benzisothiazolinone
description: Benzisothiazolinone is a biocide used as a preservative in many products (paint, detergents, varnishes, adhesives ...). It has a fungicidal effect.

description: Benzyl benzoate is an odorant molecule that is one of the allergens regulated by Europe. It is found naturally in Peru and Tolu balsams, as well as in Jasmine and Ylang-ylang essential oils. Benzyl Benzoate is part of the reference treatment in France for scabies (an infectious skin disease).
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Benzophenone-3

note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Benzyl Alcohol
description: Benzyl alcohol is one of the allergens regulated by Europe. It is mostly used as a preservative. It is naturally present in many plants and fruits, including Jasmine, garlic and Ylang-Ylang, as well as apricot, almond, apple and blackcurrant... Benzyl alcohol is authorized in organic farming.

name: Benzyl Cinnamate

It is also a UV absorber, helping to stabilize products. The first sun creams were produced using this ingredient, but it was abandoned as it was not very effective.

It is usually extracted from sugar beet or sugar cane.

name: Bht
description: BHT or Butylhydroxytoluene is a phenol which is in the form of yellowish-white crystals. It is used for its antioxidant properties in cosmetics.IARC (International Center for Research on Cancer) classifies BHT among substances whose safety could not be established, the ARTAC (French Association for Therapeutic Anti-Cancer Research), class it, among the substances "probably carcinogenic".The substance is suspected to be an endocrine disruptor that can destabilize reproductive hormones in men and women. The molecule is also known as sensitizing, which can be a source of allergies.As far as it is possible in cosmetics to do otherwise, according to the precautionary principle, we classify this molecule in red on INCI Beauty.

name: Bisabolol

It is in the form of a slightly fragrant oily liquid. It is used in cosmetics for its soothing properties, but is also known as an anti-inflammatory and healing agent. It is also for these functions (anti-irritant) that it is found in some toothpastes.

description: Butyl acetate is a solvent used in nail polish. It is used in general with ethyl acetate to help dissolve nitrocellulose well. It allows a uniform distribution of colors, plastics or film-forming agents in nail polish. It is forbidden in organic.

name: Butylene Glycol
description: Butylene Glycol is a chemical compound derived from petroleum, often used as humectant in skin care. It prevents the product from drying and makes the formulation more resistant to moisture. In sensitive individuals, butylene glycol may be irritating to skin, eyes and nasal passages, however it is considered the least irritating of all glycols. It can be authorized in organic, when it is bio-sourced and obtained by fermentation from sugar.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

description: Butylparaben is a long-chain paraben used in cosmetics as a preservative. It is suspected of being an endocrine disruptor, and even identified as such by the official scientific bodies of the main European countries: present on List I (List of substances identified as endocrine disruptors at EU level) of the EDLists website.Following the precautionary principle, and given the many doubts that persist concerning Butylparaben, the ingredient is associated with a red flower on our application.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Butyrospermum Parkii Butter
description: Shea butter is obtained from the fruit (nuts) of shea tree, a tree mainly present on the African continent. It is composed mainly of triglycerides, fatty acids, esters of wax and active ingredients: among them, vitamins (A, D, E), resinous esters, phytosterols, latex.It is used in many cosmetics for its soothing and softening properties for the epidermis. It can also protect the skin and scalp from many external aggressions.

name: Calcium Gluconate
description: Calcium gluconate is used in cosmetics as a binder and humectant. It is also used in the process of purification of wastewater. In medicine, it is used for certain pathologies related to calcium deficiency. Under the name E578, in food, it serves as a gelling agent and acidity regulator.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Calcium Hydroxide
description: Calcium hydroxide is also called "slaked lime" or "hydrated lime". This element is formed with water and lime. Limewater is the essential base ingredient for making oleo-limestone liniments. We find calcium hydroxide in hair straightening products, it has the ability to change the structure of the hair: it destroys the sulfide bonds that link the amino acids in locks of hair and changes their physical structure. Finally, it is also used in some products as a PH regulator, being basically alkaline.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride

It is used in cosmetics in a large number of products and make-up. It is also often found in home-made cosmetics useful for the manufacture of light creams, dry oils, after-sun care ... It also serves as a base for diluting and applying essential or basic oils. to make plant extracts. It is widely used for its dispersive properties and is suitable for the most sensitive skin. It is authorized in organic.

For these reasons, it is used in a multitude of cosmetic products.

name: Carbomer
description: Carbomers are hydrophilic synthetic polymers of acrylic acid. In cosmetics, they are used to thicken the preparations as a gelling agent or emulsifier, but also as a solvent to help the formulas stay well mixed. These are big molecules that do not penetrate the barriers of the skin. They improve the textures of creams or shampoos, so that they are more pleasant to use.The CIR (Cosmetic Ingredient Review) believes that carbomers are safe for health in cosmetics. Indeed, they have been the subject of many tests and do not pose in general no problems.

name: Cellulose Gum
description: Cellulose occurs naturally in the walls of plants, including in the wood. The term "cellulose gum" describes a cellulose derivative, without precising which one. Cellulose is generally extracted industrially from wood and is used to manufacture plastics (cellophane), cardboard and textiles (viscose). In cosmetics, it is a binding agent and emulsion stabiliser.In the detergent category, it acts as an anti-redeposition agent, preventing dirt from redepositing on a surface during washing.

description: Beeswax is secreted by bees and makes it possible to make "nests" , in which honey can be stored. Both are harvested at the same time by the beekeepers, who then separate them using gravity while heating them. The wax once recovered is purified for use.Beeswax is a fatty substance used in cosmetics as a thickener or emulsifier. It has protective and moisturizing properties. It forms a soft protective film on the skin,  unlike waxes derived from petroleum such as paraffin or petrolatum, and if it is formulated correctly, it does not clog the pores.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Animal.

note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

description: Cetearyl alcohol belongs to the family of fatty alcohols. It is used as emollient. It helps to thicken the creams and stabilize them. It softens and protects the skin without oily effect. Cetearyl alcohol contains mainly cetyl alcohol (Cetyl alcohol) and stearyl alcohol (Stearyl alcohol). It is authorized in organic.The CIR (Cosmetic Ingredient Review) in an annual report published in 2008, concluded the safety of fatty alcohols.

As it is a cationic surfactant, it disperses water and oil, and thus promote soft and pleasant consistencies. This ingredient is often used in hair care instead and sometimes with silicones. It can also be used as a preservative.In 2010, in a report of opinions, the Scientific Committee for Consumer Safety (CSSC) concluded that "In addition to the fact that quaternary ammonium derivative formulations have an irritating potential, especially when combinations of the compounds concerned The SCCS states that the use of CETRIMONIUM CHLORIDE, STEARTRIMONIUM CHLORIDE and BEHENTRIMONIUM CHLORIDE does not pose a risk to consumer health "when used within the regulated concentration limits (see below).

name: Ci 77491

name: Ci 77163
description: CI 77163 is a white colorant of mineral origin, also known as bismuth oxychloride (Bismoklite). It is particularly used in make-up, where it is an ingredient in powders, lipsticks, eyeshadows, mascaras and blushes. Because of its luminous effect, you'll find it in some hair products to add a shine effect.

name: Ci 77713

note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ci 77891
description: Titanium dioxide is a very abundant mineral on earth: it is the 9th most abundant chemical element in the earth's crust. It can be used in cosmetics as a UV filter or as a white dye. When it is a UV filter, it can be reduced in the form of nanoparticles: the CSSC has approved the use of titanium dioxide in the form of nanoparticles in sun creams from 2014, in concentrations of less than 25%. The EC regulation 2016/1143 of July 2016 released the use of titanium dioxide in this form in cosmetics. Titanium Dioxide reduced to the size of nanoparticles is allowed in Bio, and removes the unsightly whitish effect caused by this mineral filter when used in its micrometric state, i.e. in its original size. Please note that the manufacturer has the obligation to mention next to the name of the ingredient the mention (NANO) or [NANO] in the INCI list, when this is the case.In 2006, the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) classified titanium dioxide TiO2 in the group of substances "possible carcinogenic to humans" (group 2B): when inhaled regardless of the size of the nanoparticle or not. In 2016, ANSES submitted to ECHA (European Chemicals Agency) a proposal to classify titanium dioxide as an inhalation carcinogen (re-evaluation in category 1B).As you have understood, the case of Titanium Dioxide, the only UV filter authorized in Bio with Zinc Oxide is very complex. Nevertheless, we note that it mainly raises questions and controversies when inhaled/swallowed and/or is used in nanoparticle form.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Cocamidopropyl Betaine
description: Betaine cocamidopropyl or CAPB is amphoteric surfactant often used in shower gels and shampoos as a secondary TA. It has softened more aggressive surfactants such as sulfated anionics such as Sodium Laureth Sulfate or Sodium Lauryl Sulfate. This surfactant is made from coconut oil derivative and dimethylaminopropylamine, which does not make it a natural compound.It is one of the ingredients that is an exception in the COSMOS specifications: it is authorized in organic.

name: Coumarin
description: Coumarin is an aromatic substance that is one of the allergens regulated by Europe. Its name comes from “cumaru”, the name of the Tonka tree (in an Amazonian language). Its beans contain 1-3% coumarin. The compound was first synthesized by W.H. Perkin in 1868 (Perkin reaction). It is also found in honey, green tea and cinnamon. Its scent is reminiscent of freshly cut hay, but with many different notes and nuances (hazelnut, almond, vanilla, etc.).

name: Cyclomethicone
description: This substance is no longer authorized in cosmetics in Europe because it has been identified as a persistent organic pollutant (POP) or (very) persistent, (very) bioaccumulative and toxic (PBT/vPvB). Its use is prohibited by the Stockholm Convention on POPs or restricted by the REACH regulation.-----------------------------Cyclomethicone is a mixture of silicones D4, D5 and D6, in variable proportions. It is, like D4 and D5, suspected of being an endocrine disruptor. It is prohibited in organic products.
common_product_types: Care for dry and damaged hair (6.94%)
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Cyclopentasiloxane
description: Cyclopentasiloxane is a volatile silicone also called Silicone D5. It does not accumulate on the hair or skin and gives consumers the advantage of this family without its disadvantages: soft, non-greasy feel and lubricating properties.However, it has been closely monitored for several years and its release into the wild remains a concern. D5 is classified vPvB (very persistent and very bioaccumulative). The risk for the environment arises from its presence in certain cosmetic products rinsed with: from January 31, 2020, the maximum content of D5 will be set at 0.1% in cosmetic products rinsed with water, which fact, prohibits its use, since in such concentrations it will no longer be of any interest. It is prohibited in organic.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

description: This substance is no longer authorised in cosmetics in Europe because it has been identified as a persistent organic pollutant (POP) or (very) persistent, (very) bioaccumulative and toxic (PBT/vPvB). name: Dimethiconol
description: Dimethiconol is known to create a subtle shine, smooth and silky to the touch. It gives a "bounced" appearance to the skin temporarily, which allows it to fill imperfections and small wrinkles. Compared to other silicones, it is known to have a fluid texture and less oily, which facilitates its spread on the skin. Dimethiconol does not pose a health problem: the CIR has given it a score of 1 in 10 in terms of risk (1 representing the best of the ratings). Dimethethiconol is a large molecule that does not penetrate the skin, and is often used with Cyclopentasiloxane for better diffusion. Like all silicones, it is not very biodegradable. It is forbidden in organic.
common_product_types: Care for dry and damaged hair (23.11%)
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Dimethyl Glycyrrhizinate
description: Dimethyl Glycyrrhizate is a derivative of glycyrrhizic acid, itself derived from licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra). This salt is used in cosmetics for its soothing and skin-conditioning properties.This ingredient is not listed in the European INCI database (Cosing), but it is nevertheless used in cosmetics in Europe.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Dimethyl Ether
description: Produced by the dehydration of methanol, dimethyl ether is a colorless gas mainly used as a propellant in cosmetics, and in particular in hairsprays and hair spray.Attention, this gas should not come into contact with the eyes, and not be breathed: which seems difficult given its use in cosmetics spray.Note that it is used with propane to effectively remove warts.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Dimethicone
description: Dimethicone also called PDMS is a silicone that is not subject to any European restrictions. It is also the most used silicone in cosmetics. Its role is to produce a film of surface around the hair and on the skin, to protect them then (occlusive effect, with what that can imply). It also brings sweetness to the products and makes it easy to use creams and shampoos. It is a little "the Swiss knife of the chemist": it is used a little in all sauces, to make the products brighter, more pleasant and therefore more sellers, or to compensate for the drying effect of certain ingredients like surfactants.This inert component poses no problem for human health, one can only wonder if its effect is not only marketing and artificial. It is on the ecological aspect that the ingredient can have a very negative impact since it is very little biodegradable.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Disodium Edta
description: EDTA (EDTA and its main salts used in Disodium EDTA cosmetics, Tetrasodium EDTA, Trisodium EDTA) is a chelating agent that has been used since the 1930s and for which industrialists have complete control of processing and use. Its main property is to complex heavy metals. That is to say, it will somehow neutralize them by forming with them a complex, then serve them as carriers and evacuate them. It is therefore quite logically used in medicine to fight against heavy metal poisoning (lead for example).It is often used as a sequestrant (calcium, limestone ...) in soaps or shower gels, this allows to manage the "hard" water.The ingredient does not pose a problem for human health but its impact on the environment is disastrous: it is not biodegradable and is extremely harmful to nature. The sewage treatment plants do not hold him back and let him run into our rivers! Charcoal filters in our drinking water are not more effective at stopping it (so we are re-treating EDTA / Iron complexes). The problem is finally that he carries with him heavy metals, whose final destination is unknown.EDTA and its salts are banned in organic.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

note: Требует осторожности при использовании.

name: Ethylhexyl Salicylate
description: Ethylhexyl Salicylate is a synthetic UVB filter. It absorbs UVB radiation (from 280 to 320 nm).
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ethylenediamine
description: Ethylenediamine — косметический ингредиент. Происхождение: Synthetic.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ethylhexyl Dimethyl Paba
description: Ethylhexyl Dimethyl PABA is a chemical sunscreen authorized for use in cosmetics in Europe. It protects against UVB rays. Because of the way it is produced, it is associated with an orange flower on our application.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ethylhexyl Methoxycinnamate
description: Ethylhexyl methoxycinnamate (octinoxate) is a chemical UVB filter (280 to 320 nm) used in sunscreens. It is suspected of being an endocrine disruptor for humans and aquatic life. It is notably implicated in the bleaching of coral reefs.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ethylhexyl Triazone
description: Ethylhexyl triazone (EHT) is an organic (chemical) UV filter used in sunscreens. It absorbs UVB rays (280 to 320 nm). Aside from its less-than-virtuous chemical transformation, the ingredient is considered biodegradable (soluble) and poses no known problems once released into the environment.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ethylhexyl Palmitate
description: Ethylhexyl palmitate (octyl palmitate) is an ingredient used in cosmetics as a solvent, carrier or perfume fixative. Its possible natural origin makes it a choice alternative to silicones, since it offers a similar texture and feel on skin or hair.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Etidronic Acid
description: Etidronic acid is used as a chelating agent in cosmetics. It creates complexes with calcium, arsenic, iron and other metal ions to neutralize them. This makes it possible to manage the use of slightly "hard" waters, which could interfere with the surfactants of the product for example. Chelators are often used in water softeners. This molecule does not pose any problems for human health, its disadvantage is that, like EDTA, it is very little biodegradable, and the complexes it forms with metals are found scattered in nature.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Etocrylene
description: Etocrylene — косметический ингредиент.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике.

name: Ethylparaben
description: Ethylparaben is an ethyl ester of para-hydroxybenzoic acid used in cosmetics as a preservative. This ingredient has been suspected for several years of being an endocrine disruptor. Although it has been withdrawn in 2022 from the substances to be assessed in Europe by CORAP, in 2023 the Dutch RIVM (National Institute for Public Health and the Environment) recommends further research into exposure to multiple products containing Butylparaben and the potential danger of exposure to a combination of different parabens.Serious uncertainties therefore still exist about the safety of parabens in cosmetics, and insofar as it is possible to do otherwise, following the precautionary principle, ethylparaben is associated with a red flower on our application.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Ethylhexylglycerin
description: Ethylhexylglycerin is made from glycerine, it is used in cosmetics as an emollient humectant, it improves the skin sensation of cosmetic formulations. It is also used in deodorants as an active ingredient, it inhibits the growth and multiplication of odor-causing bacteria, while not affecting skin flora. Although it is banned in organic, because of its manufacturing process, however, it does not break any particular concerns.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Eucalyptus Globulus Leaf Oil
description: The Eucalyptus leaf has been used for hundreds of years in various remedies. For example, the plant is used in medicines to relieve the airways. In cosmetics, it is used for its flavor and antiseptic functions. It has recently been shown (Bachir, Selvakumar, 2012) that this oil may have an action in the anti-dandruff treatment of certain dandruff like Malassezia furfur. It is also effective against lice (pediculus humanus) by destroying the slow ones.It would take a whole treatise, to treat all the known benefits of this plant, however, the essential oil that contains it in pure form can be problematic, containing for example terpenes (volatile VOCs). Cineole, which is included, can also cause neuronal toxicity and convulsions at high doses.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

It also has antiseptic properties; in the United States, for example, it is used as a pesticide, classified on the list of "minimum risk pesticides." It is one of the allergens regulated by Europe.

name: Glyceryl Caprylate
description: Glyceryl caprylate is a monoester of glycerine with caprylic acid. It is mainly used as a co-emulsifier to stabilize O/W emulsions and surfactants. For its antimicrobial effects, it is most often used as a preservative, in the fight against acne to soften and moisturize. In some cases, its antimicrobial activity makes it possible to dispense with traditional preservatives.

name: Glycerin
description: Glycerol or glycerol is part of the family of polyalcohols, it can be produced synthetically from petroleum derivatives, or from animal or vegetable fats by saponification. It is in the form of a colorless liquid, hygroscopic (which absorbs moisture) and syrupy.Glycerine is used for its high moisturizing, occlusive (protects the skin of aggressions), emollient (smooth skin and hair) or humectant (allows a product to maintain its fluid appearance).It has been demonstrated (2005, Andersen) that it has anti-inflammatory power when incorporated into potentially irritating products, such as those using Sodium lauryl sulfate.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Glyceryl Rosinate
description: Glycerol rosinate is an ester of rosin and glycerol. Its high density allows it to keep oils suspended in water. Therefore, it is often used as a stabilizer in beverages and is quite common in chewing gum. In cosmetics, it is primarily used in wax for hair removal, mascaras, and lipsticks. It is permitted in organic/bio products.

name: Glyceryl Stearate
description: Glycerol stearate is used as a non-ionic emulsifier or emollient in cosmetic products. It is widely used in moisturizers and is also found in hair care products for its antistatic properties. It can be derived from palm, olive or rapeseed oil... It is  confirmed organic.

name: Glyceryl Oleate
description: The glyceryl oleate is obtained from oils and fats of natural origin. It is a fatty acid ester that has softening and softening properties, it is used as a stabilizing agent in O / W (oil / water) type emulsions. Allowed in Bio.
common_product_types: 2-in-1 shampoo and body wash for baby (32.59%)
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Glyceryl Stearate Se
description: Glyceryl Stearate SE is a monoester of glycerine and stearic acid. The term “SE” designates a ‘Special’ grade that enhances the functions of classic “Glyceryl Stearate”. Of plant origin, it has a hydrophilic-lipophilic ratio (HLB) of 5 to 8 for creating water-in-oil emulsions. It emulsifies both aqueous and oily phases. It can also be used as a co-emulsifier, acting as a stabilizer and thickener in a wide range of cosmetics.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Glycine
description: Glycine is a non-essential amino acid (produced by our bodies), not to be confused with its botanical namesake “Glycine Max”, which stands for soy, or with Glycine, a climbing plant common in our gardens, identified by its botanical name “Wisteria sinensis”,which have absolutely nothing to do with each other. In this case, glycine plays an essential role in protein synthesis, particularly that of our body's collagen. It is present in large quantities in the NMF (Natural Moisturizing Factors) of the skin. It can be used as a dietary supplement and in cosmetics, for its moisturizing, antioxidant and healing properties.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Animal.

It comes in the form of a waxy white solid cream that is used to condition the skin or hair. It is also used as a thickener and gives the creams a pearly appearance. The ingredient can come from either animal origin (cows, pigs ...) or from plants such as soybean oil and canola oil.

It is not allowed in organic.

Security."In SCCNFP, for its part concluded in a 2002 opinion on the use of peroxide in bleaching products that "The overall evidence indicates that the appropriate use of teeth whitening agents containing from 0.1 to 6, 0% - hydrogen peroxide (or its equivalent for substances releasing hydrogen peroxide) is safe if used under the supervision of a dentist. name: Hydrogenated Starch Hydrolysate
description: Used since the 1960s, hydrogenated starch hydrolysates (HSH) are a family of sweeter sweeteners than sugar, widely used in food in confectionery for example. They are produced by the partial hydrolysis of corn starch, wheat or potato. In practice, this rather generic name is used to designate any extended group of polyols (sugar alcohols).They are used in cosmetics as humectant: that is to say they retain water in the products and allow the pasta not to crystallize over time.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

description: Keratin is a fibrous protein found in hair, feathers, wool and nails. name: Isoamyl P-Methoxycinnamate
description: Isoamyl P-Methoxycinnamate (Amiloxate) is a chemical UV filter authorized in Europe, which protects against UVB rays. Listed in CORAP since 2016, the ingredient is suspected of being an endocrine disruptor (Environment), and European health authorities are still awaiting additional information!ECHA (European Chemical Industry) concluded in 2018 that additional information was required to enable the competent authority of the evaluating Member State (Federal Office for Chemicals, Germany) to complete the assessment to determine whether the substance constitutes a hazard or a risk to the environment.The concern regarding the ingredient relates to the potential for environmental endocrine disruption in non-mammalian species (amphibians). The endocrine activity of the substance in amphibians needs to be clarified to determine whether it poses a hazard and/or a risk to the environment.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Kaolin
description: Kaolin is a white clay composed of aluminum silicates. It goes into the manufacture of paper or porcelain. The clay can be used in cosmetics as an active ingredient or as a white dye (CI 77004). Kaolin is used to relieve sensitive skin, keep white teeth, absorb and regulate excess sebum or fight against perspiration. Kaolin is allowed in organic.Note that the CIR (American) has opened a new consultation (re-evaluation) of kaolin in May 2018.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Lactic Acid
description: Lactic acid is a fruit acid (AHA). It is used in cosmetics as a PH regulator, helps soften and retain moisture in the skin, and give shine. It promotes the hydration of the skin and the evacuation of dead cells. Lactic acid is produced through a fermentation process using specific bacteria such as Lactobacillus. It is naturally present in milk, wine, some vegetables and fruits. It is authorized in organic.

name: Lanolin
description: Lanolin, also called wool fat or wool wax, is obtained by purification and refining of the suint (fat part of the wool). Lanolin is used to moisturize skin, hair and nails. It acts as a lubricant of the skin, which gives it a soft and smooth appearance. It helps to form emulsions and blends well with almost any other substance used in cosmetics and personal care products. Lanolin also has adhesive characteristics.

In 2002, a patent filed by Unilever, reveals that Maltodextrin enhances the anti-aging activity of weak acids and reduces skin irritation sometimes caused by other acids.Maltodextrin is allowed in Bio.

name: Mel
description: Honey is a viscous liquid derived from the nectar of flowers and produced by bees. It is a natural sweetener that contains a mixture of glucose, fructose, sucrose and other natural sugars. As part of cosmetics, honey is used as a skin moisturizer, humectant, antibacterial and flavoring. Honey is attributed to many properties such as treating acne, having antioxidant properties or even being a hair conditioner.On the side of the antibacterial properties, it is the Manuka honey that must be favored: it is produced from the bushes of the same name in New Zealand. It contains a "Super" antibacterial called Methylglyoxal or MGO, ideal in a facial mask.Honey is allowed in organic.

name: Methylparaben
description: Methylparaben is one of the short-chain parabens. It is used as a preservative (antifungal) in cosmetics. Although long-chain parabens are reputed to be the most dangerous, in 2025 Methylparaben is the subject of a request (from France) for specific classification as an endocrine disruptor at European level (CLH).In keeping with the precautionary principle, and the many controversies surrounding it, Methylparaben is associated with a red flower on our application.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Methylpropanediol
description: Methylpropanediol (MPD) is a synthetic ingredient used in cosmetics as a solvent, i.e. for its ability to dissolve various substances. It also has moisturizing properties and can be used to enhance the efficacy of certain preservatives.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Methylisothiazolinone
description: Methylisothiazolinone, better known by the acronym MIT, is used as a synthetic preservative in cosmetics. It has been used as a substitute for parabens in so-called "paraben free" products by a number of brands. But, it turns out that the molecule is extremely allergenic, it's nerd! The American Contact Dermatitis Society even designated it in 2013 as the "ALLERGEN OF THE YEAR"!Due to many cases, European legislation has recently adapted:The ingredient has been completely banned in rinsed products since February 2017 and since April 2018, the maximum permitted levels of MIT in rinsed products have been largely revised downward (see below).MIT is mostly used with CMIT (Methylchloroisothiazolinone).
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

description: The restrictions depend on the use of the ingredient in:a) Oxidative dye for hair colouringb) Non-oxidative dye for hair colouringThe maximum permitted concentrations are as follows:a) From 3 September 2017, after mixing under oxidative conditions, the maximum concentration applied to the hair must not exceed 1.0%.b) From 3 September 2017: 1.5%For a) and b), from 3 September 2017:- do not use with nitrosating agents,- maximum nitrosamine content: 50 μg/kg- store in nitrite-free containers.Wording of conditions of use and warnings:a) From 3 March 2018 must appear on the label:- The mixing ratio.- Hair dyes can cause severe allergic reactions.- Read and follow directions.- This product is not intended for use on persons under 16 years of age.- Temporary black henna tattoos may increase the risk of allergy.Do not color your hair if:- you have a rash on your face or if your scalp is sensitive, irritated or damaged.- you have had a reaction to coloring your hair in the past.- you have had a reaction to a temporary black henna tattoo in the past.

name: Nitrocellulose
description: Nitrocellulose or cellulose nitrate is a cellulose derivative. It is used in cosmetics, exclusively as a film-forming agent in nail polish and care.Cellulose is present in plants in large quantities. It is nitrated (originally with nitric acid, but today a mixture of sulfuric acid and nitric acid is used to accelerate the transformation) to form an ester, nitrocellulose, which is then treated with alcohol or water and sold as a moist powder. It is prohibited in Bio.Nitrocellulose in the dry state is a powerful explosive (at least as powerful as TNT). It is particularly implicated in the explosion of hangar 221 of the AZF factory. It was manufactured by the Toulouse National Gunnery during the First World War, and its residues could explain the explosion of 21 September 2001.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Olaflur
description: Olaflur is a fluorine salt of the organic type (amine fluoride) used since the end of the 1960s in toothpastes and oral hygiene products. With a PH that is more acidic than inorganic fluoride, it is more effective in the re-mineralization of tooth enamel. However, its manufacturing cost being much higher and its non-compatibility with abrasives derived from calcium, make it found in barely 5% of cosmetic oral hygiene products. It is forbidden in organic.Surprisingly, the ingredient is not subject to any European regulation. However, it is a fluoride whose rate must not exceed (1500 ppm F) or 0.15% of the total ingredients.

name: Palmitic Acid
description: Palmitic acid is one of the most common saturated fatty acids and one of the most common in body lipids: it accounts for 21 to 30% of human fat tissue (fat). Palmitic acid is used for many functions in cosmetic products: surfactant (detergent), emulsifier (allows mixing of oil and water), opacifying agent or emollient (softens the skin). It is authorized for use in organic products.

name: Paraffinum Liquidum
description: Paraffin oil is used in cosmetics as a softening and film-forming agent: it is occlusive, and creates a film to protect the skin. Paraffin oil is a hydrocarbon derivative (petroleum, coal). It is found in many products such as moisturizers, lip balms or makeup.Criticism focuses on its origin, its very low biodegradability (pollution following its release into the wild) and its high occlusive power. It should be preferred oils of vegetal origins more neutral.

name: Parfum
description: Unfortunately, the term “fragrance” conceals numerous odorant molecules that are potentially irritating or even allergenic. It's hard to know what to do when you're allergic to a product and the information isn't very detailed. Nevertheless, to help us, Europe has regulated 24 molecules (more than 80 from 2026) which, when used above a certain concentration, must be mentioned in the INCI list (generally at the end), even though they are themselves part of a product's fragrance. This is the case, for example, with Limonene, linalool...
note: Low penalty in all categories. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Panthenol
description: Panthenol is an alcohol widely used in cosmetics. This active ingredient goes into the formulation of shampoos and conditioners to make hair shiny and supple. Panthenol improves hydration, reduces itching and inflammation of the skin. It accelerates and improves the healing of epidermal wounds. It is often used in sunburn treatment products. When panthenol is applied topically, it penetrates the lower layers of the skin, is absorbed by skin cells and converted to pantothenic acid (more commonly known as vitamin B5). It is this penetration that allows essential hydration.The ingredient does not exist in its natural state in this form is prohibited in Bio, it's a shame!In a study published in December 2017, the CIR concluded that panthenol poses no health risk in cosmetics. However, other studies report contact irritations and allergies, but in much larger doses than those used in cosmetics.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Peg-100 Stearate
description: PEG-100 Stearate is an ester of Polyethylene Glycol and Stearic Acid. The origin of this synthetic ingredient is therefore a plastic material. It is used mainly in cosmetics as emulsifier, emollient and surfactant. It is in the form of a soft solid of white to beige color. In addition to its cleaning capabilities, it gives products a velvety texture.Due to its ethoxylation pollutant production, PEGs and their derivatives are banned in organic form.In a study published in the journal Toxicology in 2005 titled "Assessment of the safety of polyethylene glycols (PEGs) and their derivatives in cosmetics", it was concluded that: "Taking into account all available information from On the basis of the available data, it is therefore concluded that PEGs from a wide range of molecular weights (200 to over 10,000), their ethers (laureth, ceteth, ceteareth, steareth and oleth) and fatty acids (laurate, dilaurate, stearate, distearate) are safe for cosmetics. "However, according to other studies of the same newspaper, it is also mentioned that PEG may contain impurities harmful to health including ethylene oxide, 1,4 dioxane, iron, hydrocarbons polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) or arsenic. It is advisable to avoid this type of ingredient if you have sensitive skin.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Phenoxyethanol
description: Phenoxyethanol is a synthetic preservative known by the acronym of EGPhE, widely used in cosmetics and increasingly controversial. Since 2012, the ANSM (National Agency for the Safety of Medicines and Health Products) has recommended that this preservative not be used in cosmetic products intended for babies' seats and that its maximum content be set at 0.4% for other products for children under 3 years old. It also denounces its liver toxicity, its potentially harmful effects on reproduction as well as many other inconveniences: the conclusions of the 2012 ANSM report are alarming.In its opinion of May 2018, the Temporary Scientific Special Committee (CSST) responsible for the re-evaluation of phenoxyethanol at the European level, for its part concluded: "The recommendation by the Ansm of non-use of phenoxyethanol in products It is advisable to extend it to wipes, which are very commonly used also for cleaning the seats of young children.In all other cosmetic products intended for children aged 3 and under, the maximum concentration of Phenoxyethanol could remain 1%. " The CSST would therefore follow the ANSM for wipes but not for concentration. In any case, by law, phenoxyethanol is still allowed in baby wipes!Phenoxyethanol is produced by ethoxylation (a highly polluting chemical process) by reacting phenol and ethylene oxide at high temperature and pressure. Note, however, that phenoxyethanol is naturally present in green tea, but it is not this version of the compound that is used in cosmetics. Due to its manufacturing process, Phenoxyethanol is banned in Bio.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Propane
description: Propane is a colorless, flammable gas used as a propellant, solvent and extractor at concentrations <1000 ppm. It is non-toxic, at 100,000 ppm, it causes mild dizziness but does not irritate the nose or throat. It is a high concentration asphyxiant. It is low emitter of CO2 and is non-irritant.

name: Propylene Glycol
description: Propylene Glycol is an organic compound (Dialcohol) made by hydrating propylene oxide. It occurs naturally in sesame seeds and mushrooms. It is used as a humectant, solvent, or binder in cosmetic products. It helps other ingredients penetrate the skin. Propylene Glycol is the main ingredient in electronic cigarettes.Propanediol (1,3-propanediol) and propylene glycol (1,2-propanediol) share the same empirical formula (C3H8O2), but their molecular structure is different.

name: Propylparaben

It is also present in foodstuffs (E216) for the same purpose (jams, drinks, sauces, etc.).Although the European Scientific Committee states in its latest opinion of 2021: "Available data on propylparaben provide indications of its potential endocrine effects. However, the current level of evidence is not sufficient to consider it as an endocrine disrupting substance, nor to establish a toxicological starting point based on its endocrine disrupting properties, usable in human health risk assessment", we believe, insofar as it is possible not to use it and therefore to do otherwise, and applying the precautionary principle, that this ingredient should be avoided in cosmetic products. For these reasons, it is accompanied by a red flower on our application.

name: Resorcinol
description: Resorcinol is used as a coloring in hair dyes (ColorIndex: CI 76505). However, "it is the rubber industry that is the largest user of resorcinol (about 50%) and it is also used for the manufacture of high quality wood glues (about 25%). Other uses include dye manufacture, pharmaceuticals, flame retardants, agrochemicals, fungicide creams and lotions, and hair dyes. " (WHO source)On a notice published in 2013, the SCCS (Europe) concluded that "Resorcinol is a powerful sensitizer."Studies suspect the substance of being an endocrine disruptor:In humans, thyroid effects, CNS disorders and erythrocyte abnormalities have been attributed to resorcinol exposure Skin sensitization to resorcinol is well known but rare in practice, and the available data cannot assess the effective sensitizing action of this coumpound. " (WHO source)
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах.

name: Retinol
description: Retinol is produced synthetically, but is naturally present in animal fats, fish liver oil and plants containing beta-carotene. Retinol is a natural form of vitamin A, which works by reducing the appearance of wrinkles and increasing the thickness and elasticity of the skin.It is prohibited in organic products.

description: Castor oil is a vegetable oil extracted from seeds, it is mainly made up of fatty acids (mostly ricinoleic acid). This oil is used because of its numerous properties in cosmetics: it promotes the uniform distribution of pigments in a cosmetic product, it penetrates the stratum corneum and acts in depth, it moisturizes and protects. In hair care, it has toning properties and protects the hair (film-forming agent). It is also very used in lip care, it makes them soft and gives them shine. In makeup, it is used to promote a good distribution of colors. It is allowed in organic.

name: Salicylic Acid
description: Salicylic acid is naturally present in many fruits or certain plants such as willow bark. It is used in cosmetics as a preservative, hair conditioner or for its smoothing and regenerating effects. Salicylic acid is also often used as a keratolytic in anti-dandruff shampoos, particularly for the treatment of oily dandruff (steatoid pityriasis).In an opinion published in September 2018, the European Scientific Committee on Consumer Safety (SCCS) concludes that salicylic acid is not a problem if used under the regulatory conditions (see below), however it states that: 'This opinion does not apply to products that are administered orally such as toothpastes and mouthwashes. Products to be sprayed that could result in exposure of the consumer's lung by inhalation are also excluded". Salicylic acid is allowed in Organic.Salicylic acid is classified CMR category 2: Suspected CMR effect, but insufficient information is available.* CMR means Carcinogenic, Mutagenic and Reprotoxic
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Natural.

name: Silica
description: Silica is a naturally occurring mineral in sandstone, clay and granite, as well as in some parts of plants and animals. It is used for various reasons in cosmetics: abrasive in toothpastes, opacifying in powder and blush, or absorbing in deodorants. Silica is allowed in Bio.

name: Silica Dimethyl Silylate
description: Silica Dimethyl Silylate is a hydrophobic fumed silica used in cosmetics for its thickening properties. It can be used to form transparent gels with oils such as mineral oils, or vegetable oils such as castor oil. It is widely used to improve the look and hold of lipsticks, and in certain hair care products, such as dry shampoos, it enhances product adhesion and improves volume.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике.

It is also used to reduce volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.

name: Sodium Chloride
description: Sodium chloride is a sodium salt also known as table salt, commonly used in food for salting food. It is the basic material in the chemical industry to produce soda and chlorine. In cosmetics, it is often used as a thickener in shampoos and shower gels or in oral preparations to clean and eliminate odors. Table salt (depending on the concentration used) may tend in some cases to dry the skin and irritate the scalp. Sodium chloride is allowed in organic.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Fluoride
description: Sodium fluoride is an inorganic type fluorine salt used in oral hygiene products for the prevention of cavities. In this range, it is also the most used ingredient in toothpastes and mouthwashes.The fluoride concentration should not exceed 1500 ppm (F) in a "cosmetic" toothpaste. However, this dose can be bypassed if the product is a medicine and is subject to a Marketing Authorization (MA), in which case it can only be purchased in pharmacies.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

description: Sodium Cocoyl Glutamate is made from coconut oil fatty acids and an amino acid, glutamate (present in the structural protein (collagen) of human skin). It is a rather mild anionic surfactant, used in organic formulas.
common_product_types: 2-in-1 shampoo and body wash for baby (12.65%)
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Hydroxide
description: Caustic soda is used in the preparation of many products (detergents, explosives ...). It is used to make sodium salts and to neutralize acids. In cosmetics, it allows the manufacture of solid soaps. Caustic soda is allowed in organic, it does not pose problems in the conditions of regulation.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Dehydroacetate
description: Sodium dehydroacetate is a preservative used in cosmetics for its antimicrobial properties. Although the ingredient seems to pose few health problems, note that its concentration is limited and that it is forbidden in aerosol-type products. It is permitted in organic products.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Hypochlorite
description: Sodium hypochlorite is a chemical compound often used in aqueous solution as a disinfectant and bleaching agent. He enters the composition of the bleach.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Hyaluronate
description: Sodium hyaluronate is the salt of hyaluronic acid. This active ingredient is appreciated for its moisturizing and plumping effect on the skin. It is a natural component of the connective tissue. It easily penetrates the epidermis and is then able to fix a large amount of water, up to 20 times its weight. For this reason, it is found in many cosmetics such as anti-aging, moisturizing creams or even foundations ...Discovered in the 1930s, hyaluronic acid is abundant in the dermis of young skin, but its quantity decreases with age: the skin appears more damaged, with wrinkles. Hyaluronic acid is a fairly large molecule that has difficulty penetrating the skin, and is much more effective injection. In a cosmetic, it is its salt that is used: sodium hyaluronate is a much smaller molecule, which penetrates more easily and can then move into the deep layers of the skin.This salt is a powerful humectant that attracts and holds water to it, making it the moisturizer of choice for the skin: it helps keep it "wet". As it attracts and binds to the water, it causes a slight swelling which reduces the appearance of lines and wrinkles, to give the skin a younger appearance.It is authorized in Bio.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Jojobate
description: Sodium Jojobate — косметический ингредиент. Происхождение: Vegetal.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Lactate
description: Sodium lactate is the sodium salt of lactic acid, a type of alpha-hydroxy acid (AHA). It is produced by the fermentation of sugars, e.g. from corn or sugar beet. It contains no milk proteins. It can therefore be used by people with a milk allergy or lactose intolerance. It is used as a food additive under the name E325. It is used in cosmetics for its antioxidant, antimicrobial and moisturizing properties.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate
description: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate or SCI is a mild anionic surfactant without sulfate. Derived from the fatty acids of coconut oil and isethionic acid, it has a very good foaming power. It is found in natural products such as solid shampoos.SCI is produced by ethoxylation, which makes it a not very ecological ingredient. It is prohibited in the COSMOS specifications.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
description: Sodium Laureth Sulphate (SLES) is an anionic surfactant used in cosmetics for its detergent properties. It is therefore very present in shower gels and shampoos. It is produced from Sodium Lauryl Sulphate (SLS) by ethoxylation, a very polluting chemical transformation, which is why it is prohibited in organic products. Sodium Laureth Sulphate is reputed to be less irritating than Sodium Lauryl Sulphate although the latter is more natural and authorized in Organic, it is the chemical transformation that has softened it and increased its foaming power.This ingredient is classified as ecotoxic according to REACH (Aquatic Chronic 3).
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer-5
description: Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer-5 — косметический ингредиент.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Glutamate
description: Sodium Lauroyl Glutamate — косметический ингредиент. Происхождение: Vegetal.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Hydroxymethylglycinate
description: Sodium hydroxymethylglycinate is used as a preservative in cosmetics as an alternative to parabens. It acts on its own on a fairly broad spectrum of microbes and bacteria of all kinds. Nevertheless, some studies show that it can release formaldehyde (known carcinogen, irritant and allergen) under certain conditions which, let's face it, seem infrequent. Moreover, when this is the case, the quantities released are negligible. However, considering that it is possible to do otherwise, we classify this ingredient in red on Inci Beauty. The ingredient is reputed to be very irritating to the skin and eyes, and seems to give off a very strong rancid odor even below the concentrations authorized by Europe. This leads manufacturers to dilute it too much and therefore reduce its preservative effect or to use too many masking agents.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Lactylate
description: Sodium Lauroyl Lactylate — косметический ингредиент.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике.

name: Sodium Laurate
description: Sodium Laurate is a chemical compound obtained by saponification of lauric acid (contained in coconut oil, palm oil, etc.), i.e. by reacting it with caustic soda. It is used as a basic ingredient in the composition of cosmetic solid soaps.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Sarcosinate
description: Lauroyl Sarconisate sodium is a much milder anionic surfactant than sulfated compounds. Derived from fatty acid and natural amine, it is used in the composition of mild cleansing products and is also used in toothpastes.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate
description: Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate or SLMI is a mild anionic surfactant derived from coconut, which contains no sulphate. Unlike SCI (Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate), which allows for the creation of opaque products, SLMI has excellent water solubility and therefore allows for the creation of sulfate-free, transparent shampoo formulations. It is often used in conjunction with CAPB.SLMI is produced by ethoxylation, which makes it an environmentally unfriendly ingredient. It is prohibited in the COSMOS specifications.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate
description: Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate is the salt of the coconut fatty acid amide of N-methyltaurine. It is a mild surfactant that does not pose a priori any particular problem.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Monofluorophosphate
description: Sodium Monofluorophosphate or more simply Sodium Fluorophosphate is an inorganic fluoride salt used in toothpastes and oral hygiene products. It helps prevent cavities. It is used as a replacement for Sodium Fluoride, however, no study today can prove increased effectiveness compared to the latter.The fluoride concentration should not exceed 1500 ppm (F) in a "cosmetic" type toothpaste. However, this dose can be circumvented if the product is a medicine and is the subject of a marketing authorization (MA), in which case it can only be purchased in pharmacies.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Moringate
description: Sodium Moringate — косметический ингредиент. Происхождение: Vegetal.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Lauryl Glucose Carboxylate
description: Sodium Lauryl Glucose Carboxylate — косметический ингредиент.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике.

name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
description: Sodium Lauryl sulfate is a surfactant widely used in cosmetics. It is produced mostly from palm oil. It has a very strong foaming and detergent power, but it is criticized for its very irritating and drying effect on the skin, so much so that it is found less and less often in shower gels and bath products. Manufacturers prefer to use the ethoxylated version (SLES = Sodium Laureth Sulfate) less abrasive or other softer surfactants. However, it remains very present in toothpastes for adults (57% contain it) and children (42%).SLS is also criticized for being a pollutant for the environment, animals, aquatic environments or even plants. Nevertheless, it is authorized in organic.It is classified according to REACH: Ecotoxic (Aquatic Chronic 3).

It has been shown to have a stronger moisturizing power than glycerine, sorbitol or propanediol. This ingredient is also non-toxic, non-irritating and non-allergic.

name: Sorbitol
description: Sorbitol is a polyol that comes in the form of a white, sweet crystalline powder. It is used primarily as a humectant in dental hygiene products, to help the paste retain its fluid appearance after opening, but also for its non-cariogenic sweetening power (does not generate cavities like sugar). It is quite frequently found for the same reasons in moisturizing creams. In food, it is found as an additive under the name E420 or as a sweetener for diabetics.Industrially, Sorbitol is obtained, for example, by the hydrogenation of corn or glucose syrup. It is found naturally present in many fruits such as plums, apples and cherries.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sorbitan Olivate

name: Squalane
description: Squalane is an oil that has many affinities with the skin: it is one of the main components of sebum and the hydrolipidic film. It is also emollient and moisturizing. Vegetable squalane, which is sometimes called phytosqualane, generally comes from the unsaponifiable part of olive oil.Controversies exist about it when it is of animal origin and extracted from shark liver: the shark is killed, its viscera are extracted and it is thrown back into the sea.We hope that these practices are less and less common: make sure that the one used in your product is of plant origin. If it's not clear, call the brand to confirm. Squalane is produced by hydrogenation of squalene.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sorbitol/Sebacic Acid Copolymer Behenate
description: Sorbitol/Sebacic Acid Copolymer Behenate — косметический ингредиент. Происхождение: Vegetal.

note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Squalene
description: Squalene oil can be of vegetable (olive oil), animal (shark liver oil) or synthetic origin. Controversies exist when squalene is of animal origin, and extracted from the liver of sharks: the animal is killed, its viscera are extracted and its carcass is thrown back into the sea.When it is of plant origin, squalene can be called phytosqualene. Make sure it is of vegetable origin in your products, do not hesitate to contact the brand to make sure. However, most squalene used today is no longer of animal origin.Squalene is used primarily as an emollient in many creams. Squalane is a purified and hydrogenated form of squalene, much more used in cosmetics.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Stannous Fluoride
description: Tin Fluoride is an inorganic fluoride salt used in toothpastes and oral hygiene products. It prevents cavities and is effective against gingivitis and periodontitis. It is one of the best fluoride salts to kill plaque bacteria, however, its somewhat astringent metallic taste and propensity to "leave stains on the teeth" makes it a favorite today. sodium fluoride.The fluoride concentration should not exceed 1500 ppm (F) in a "cosmetic" toothpaste. However, this dose can be bypassed if the product is a medicine and is subject to a Marketing Authorization (MA), in which case it can only be purchased in pharmacies.
note: Low penalty in all categories. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Stearalkonium Hectorite
description: The stearalkonium hectorite present itself in the form of a white and cream powder. It facilitates the dispersion of solids in liquids. It is a suspending agent. It is found in many products, such as makeup (nail polish, foundations or lipsticks).
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Stearamidopropyl Dimethylamine
description: Stearamidopropyl Dimethylamine is a cationic surfactant used as a substitute for silicones in hair care products. It combines quite easily with the hair (negatively charged) to form a smooth film, but while not occlusive as a silicone. It adheres to the fabric surface to reduce static electricity present. It is a hair conditioner that becomes quaternary ammonium in acidic medium. Given its unecological manufacturing process, the ingredient is not authorized in France but in Germany Yes (BDIH).The Cosmetic Ingredient Review (CIR) concluded in 2014 the safety of Stearamidopropyl Dimethylamine.
common_product_types: Care for dry and damaged hair (12.46%)
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Talc
description: Talc is a mineral powder widely used in blushes, foundation powders and even eye shadows. It is also used on the skin as a moisture absorber, hence its use on baby's bottom. In a cream or balm, it also helps reduce the feeling of oiliness.Several controversies exist about it:- in 2012, ANSES indicated that it was not possible to exclude the presence of asbestos fibers in certain Talc deposits.- some studies have also demonstrated irritation in children by inhalationIn 2016, Johnson & Johnson was ordered to pay $72 million to a family whose wife died of ovarian cancer and who had used talcum powder daily for 35 years on her genitals: The court n therefore not excluding that his cancer is due to this use. Since then, other trials have taken place.In July 2018, Johnson & Johnson was ordered to pay $4.69 billion in damages. 22 women and their families accused the brand that their wipes, composed of talc, had caused the ovarian cancers to which they suffered.During a recent study from December 2018 in Canada, scientists warned of the risks linked to inhalation and contact of Talc with the female genitals.For these reasons, and to the extent that it is possible to do otherwise, we have therefore decided to place this ingredient in red, if it is used in intimate hygiene products.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Tetrasodium Edta
description: EDTA (EDTA and its main salts used in Disodium EDTA cosmetics, Tetrasodium EDTA, Trisodium EDTA) is a chelating agent that has been used since the 1930s and for which industrialists have complete control of processing and use. Its main property is to complex heavy metals. That is to say, it will somehow neutralize them by forming with them a complex, then serve them as carriers and evacuate them. It is therefore quite logically used in medicine to fight against heavy metal poisoning (lead for example).It is often used as a sequestrant (calcium, limestone ...) in soaps or shower gels, this allows to manage the "hard" water.The ingredient does not pose a problem for human health but its impact on the environment is disastrous: it is not biodegradable and is extremely harmful to nature. The sewage treatment plants do not hold him back and let him run into our rivers! Charcoal filters in our drinking water are not more effective at stopping it (so we are re-treating EDTA / Iron complexes). The problem is finally that he carries with him heavy metals, whose final destination is unknown.EDTA and its salts are banned in organic.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

description: Tetrasodium Etidronate is a close cousin of EDTA. It is used in cosmetics as a chelator and stabilizer. It allows the products to remain stable as much in terms of their color as their consistency. It fights as a chelator against limescale that can limit the action of surfactants. It is like EDTA little biodegradable.

description: Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate is a synthetic chelating agent. Often proposed as an alternative to EDTA, its method of manufacture remains problematic for the environment: it uses cyanomethylation with highly toxic ingredients such as hydrogen cyanide and formaldehyde. For this reason, the molecule is accompanied by an orange flower on our application, which is exploded since the ingredient is biodegradable (high solubility over a wide PH range).

name: Tin Oxide
description: Tin oxide (CI 77861) is often used in cosmetics for its pigments, which can give a product a multi-colored effect, depending on the angle from which it is viewed. It is a mineral compound found naturally in cassiterite.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Titanium Dioxide
description: Titanium dioxide is a very abundant mineral on earth: it is in fact the 9th most abundant chemical element in the earth's crust. It can be used in cosmetics as a UV filter or as a white colorant. When it is a UV filter, it can be reduced in the form of nanoparticles: the CSSC approved the use of titanium dioxide in the form of nanoparticles in sun creams from 2014, in concentrations less than 25%. EC Regulation 2016/1143 of July 2016 freed the use of Titanium dioxide in this form in cosmetics. Titanium Dioxide reduced to the size of nanoparticles is authorized in Organic, and makes it possible to eliminate the unsightly whitish effect caused by this mineral filter when it is used in its micrometric state, that is to say in its original size. Please note, however, that the manufacturer is required to mention (NANO) or [NANO] next to the name of the ingredient in the INCI list, when this is the case.In 2006, the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) classified titanium dioxide TiO2 in the group of substances "possible carcinogens in humans" (group 2B): when inhaled regardless of the size of the Nano particle or not. In 2016, ANSES submitted to ECHA (European Chemicals Agency) a proposal for the classification of titanium dioxide as a carcinogen by inhalation (re-evaluation in category 1B).As you have understood, the case of Titanium Dioxide, the only UV filter authorized in Bio with Zinc oxide, is very complex. However, we note that it mainly raises questions and controversies when it is inhaled/swallowed and/or is used in the nanoparticle state.Titanium Dioxide (E171) has been banned since January 1, 2020 in France in food products, which is why we display it in red in dental hygiene products.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Mineral.

name: Tocopherol
description: Tocopherol represents different components of Vitamin E. It is used in cosmetics for its antioxidant properties. This fat-soluble ingredient (which dissolves in oil) is found naturally in vegetable oils such as palm oil, sunflower oil or wheat germ ... In 2014, Tocopherol made the another ERC study and 13 other vitamin E derivatives. The agency concluded that it is safe. Note that Tocopherol can be of synthetic origin (made entirely in the laboratory).
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.
